title: Text-to-Speech vs. Text-to-Music
concept: Text-to-speech converts written text into spoken audio matching that text; text-to-music generates audio matching a style or mood description, with no fixed 'correct' output.

exercises.prompt: If you type 'Hello, how are you?' into an app and it reads it out loud in a voice, what did the app just do?
exercises.explain: That's text-to-speech! The app read your exact words out loud, matching them word for word.

cards.title: Two Very Different Voices
cards.body: Ask an AI to read you a bedtime story, and it speaks your exact words. Ask an AI to make you a 'chill lo-fi beat,' and it creates something totally new every time. Same idea (text turns into sound) but two very different jobs!

cards.title: Two Kinds of Text-to-Audio
cards.body: Text-to-speech (TTS) takes written words and turns them into a spoken voice saying those exact words. Text-to-music takes a description (like 'happy piano tune') and creates audio that matches the style or mood, but there's no single 'correct' song, it makes up something new each time.

cards.title: Reading a Script vs. Making Up a Tune
cards.body: Text-to-speech is like an actor reading a script word-for-word, exactly as written. Text-to-music is like a musician told 'play something sad and slow,' who then improvises their own melody. The mood is right, but the notes are never exactly the same twice!

cards.title: Same Words, Different Tools
cards.body: Type 'Once upon a time...' into a TTS app, and it speaks those exact words aloud. Type 'make upbeat birthday music' into a music AI, and it composes a brand-new happy tune. No two 'birthday music' requests will sound identical, but every TTS reading of the same sentence sounds basically the same.

cards.title: Myth: Both Have One 'Right' Answer
cards.body: Myth: Text-to-music should sound exactly the same every time, just like text-to-speech. Truth: Text-to-speech aims to match your exact text every time. Text-to-music has no fixed correct output. It's creative, so it can generate many different songs that all fit the same mood description.

cards.title: Speech vs. Music Generation
cards.body: Compare how each system matches its output to the input.

left.text: Matches the exact written words, one clear correct-sounding result

right.text: Matches a style or mood, endless creative results possible

options.text: It turns written words into a voice speaking those exact words
It turns a mood description into a brand-new song
It has no fixed correct output
It never matches the input text

exercises.explain: Text-to-speech reads out the exact text you gave it, aiming for one accurate spoken version.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Every time you ask a text-to-music AI for 'relaxing music,' it should create the exact same song.
exercises.explain: False! Text-to-music has no single correct output, it can create many different songs that all match the 'relaxing' mood.

exercises.sentence: Text-to-speech matches your exact words, but text-to-music matches a ___ or mood instead.
exercises.explain: Text-to-music generates audio that fits a described style or mood, not exact matching text.

exercises.prompt: You type 'sad piano music' into an AI three separate times and get three different songs. What does this tell you?

options.text: The AI is broken because it should always give the same song
This is normal for text-to-music, since there's no single correct output
The AI secretly used text-to-speech instead

exercises.explain: Getting different results each time is expected with text-to-music, it creates new audio to fit the mood, not one fixed 'correct' answer.

exercises.prompt: Your friend says: 'Text-to-speech and text-to-music work exactly the same way, they both just create one correct sound for any input.' What's wrong with this statement?

options.text: Nothing, they both always produce identical results
Text-to-speech aims for one matching result for the exact text, but text-to-music can create many valid results for the same mood description
Text-to-music always matches exact words, while text-to-speech is creative
Both tools ignore the text completely and just make random sounds

exercises.explain: The key difference: text-to-speech tries to accurately voice your exact words, while text-to-music creatively generates audio fitting a mood or style, with no single correct output.
